project.description:
Homing's Problem Realm — the typed, single-parent taxonomy of the problems the studio's presentation actually solves, modelled on rebar's rigid-levels problem-realm-core (L0_Domain / L1_Domain / L2_Domain / LeafDomain). Concrete instances live here; the abstraction (the level ladder, the DomainNode contract, Desc) is rebar's job — this module never redefines it.
Two areas. (1) Presentation surfaces (realm.presentation, from the S0 audit): SiteChrome / CatalogueOrganization / DocumentPresentation / ProgressTracking. (2) Subject domains (realm.plan): PlanTracking with its functional aspects (Objectives / Phases / OpenQuestions / Acceptance / Progress / Tasks / Dependencies / Metrics), plus the parked canonical Status dimension + PhaseState value set — the genuinely domain-bound status the pilot expresses (distinct from the borrowed generic status vocabulary, which was retired).
This module is the PREREQUISITE for Expression Intents: an intent is a (usage x problem-concept x value) cell, so the problem nodes must exist and be typed before the intents that bind to them can be authored. Depends only on rebar problem-realm-core (which brings rigid-levels-core transitively); a clean leaf, nothing framework-specific.
